info.name: rfq_manager_ms — Full Postman Walkthrough Demo V2.0
info.description:
End-to-end Postman walkthrough for `rfq_manager_ms`, designed for a boss/demo session and for repeatable testing.

What this collection gives you:
- A continuous boss-demo storyline with no manual UUID copy/paste.
- Environment variables auto-populated from live responses.
- Runtime dates and unique names generated automatically at setup.
- Baseline read checks, full controlled RFQ lifecycle, guardrails, workspace actions, reminders, export.
- Optional advanced tricks: invalid payload, skip stages, stage overrides, blocker flow.
- Optional diagnostics: seeded data consistency and status-label normalization.

Recommended run order:
0 → 1 → 2 (optional advanced) → 3 (optional diagnostics)

Important notes:
- Set `base_origin`, `base_url`, and `health_url` in the environment if your server is not localhost:8000.
- Set `demo_upload_file_path` in the environment before running the upload request.
- This suite assumes the server is running and the DB is seeded with workflows and reminder rules.
- Dynamic variables are namespaced so the same collection can be rerun without manual editing.

item.name: 0 — Bootstrap & Baseline (Run First)
item.description: Prepare runtime variables, capture workflow/template IDs, grab one seeded RFQ for diagnostics, and sanity-check baseline KPI/analytics endpoints.

request.description: Runs the health endpoint and auto-generates all runtime variables needed later: unique suffix, demo RFQ names, future deadlines, reminder due date.

exec: const plusDays = days => { const d = new Date(now); d.setDate(d.getDate() + days); return fmt(d); };
console.log('Runtime demo variables bootstrapped with suffix:', suffix);

request.description: Captures `workflow_long_id` and `workflow_short_id` from the seeded workflow list.

item.name: [SETUP] Get GHI-LONG workflow → save template stage IDs

request.description: Captures long-workflow template stage IDs (`tpl_long_s1` ... `tpl_long_s11`). These template IDs are used in create-RFQ payloads for `skip_stages` and `stage_overrides`. They are NOT RFQ live stage IDs.

exec: console.log('Saved tpl_long template stages:', wf.stages.map(s => s.order + ':' + s.name).join(' | '));

item.name: [SETUP] Get GHI-SHORT workflow → save template stage IDs

request.description: Captures short-workflow template stage IDs (`tpl_short_s1` ... `tpl_short_s6`).

exec: console.log('Saved tpl_short template stages:', wf.stages.map(s => s.order + ':' + s.name).join(' | '));

item.name: [BASELINE] List RFQs → save one seeded RFQ for diagnostics

request.description: Baseline system snapshot. Saves one non-demo / non-IF / non-IB RFQ into `seeded_rfq_id` for the optional diagnostics folder.

exec: pm.test('Paginated RFQ list shape is present', () => {
console.log('Seeded RFQ selected for diagnostics:', seeded.rfq_code, seeded.id);
pm.test('At least one seeded RFQ available for diagnostics', () => pm.expect(pm.environment.get('seeded_rfq_id')).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty);

request.description: Validates the stats endpoint shape: total_rfqs_12m, open_rfqs, critical_rfqs, avg_cycle_days.
Validates the analytics endpoint shape: margins, estimation_accuracy, win_rate, by_client.

item.name: [BASELINE] Reminder rules → save useful rule IDs

request.description: Captures useful reminder-rule IDs (for example `external_followup_rule_id`) for later toggling demos.

item.name: 1 — Continuous Boss Demo | Controlled RFQ Story
item.description: This is the main demo storyline. Run top to bottom. It creates one controlled RFQ on GHI-SHORT and drives it through lifecycle, guardrails, workspace actions, reminders, metadata update, and export — all while auto-capturing IDs in the environment.

request.description: Creates the main controlled RFQ for the demo. No manual IDs are needed — the workflow ID comes from Setup.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{demo_rfq_name}}",
  "client": "{{demo_client_name}}",
  "deadline": "{{demo_deadline_short}}",
  "owner": "Mohamed Guidara",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_short_id}}",
  "industry": "Industrial Automation",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "normal",
  "description": "Controlled boss-demo RFQ created from Postman walkthrough",
  "code_prefix": "IF",
  "stage_overrides": []
}

exec: pm.test('RFQ code generated with IF prefix', () => pm.expect(rfq.rfq_code).to.match(/^IF-/));
pm.test('Initial status is In preparation', () => pm.expect(rfq.status).to.eql('In preparation'));
pm.test('Initial current stage is RFQ received', () => pm.expect(rfq.current_stage_name).to.eql('RFQ received'));

request.description: Shows that the newly created RFQ is discoverable via the portfolio/list endpoint.

exec: pm.test('Created demo RFQ can be found by client search', () => pm.expect(!!found).to.be.true);

request.description: Loads the full RFQ detail and refreshes `demo_current_stage_id` from the authoritative aggregate view.

exec: pm.test('Demo RFQ detail matches runtime vars', () => {

item.name: [DEMO-04] List demo stages → save live stage IDs

request.description: Captures live stage IDs (`demo_s1_id` ... `demo_s5_id`) so the rest of the walkthrough stays copy/paste free.

exec: stages.forEach(s => { pm.environment.set('demo_s' + s.order + '_id', s.id); pm.environment.set('demo_s' + s.order + '_name', s.name); });
pm.test('Stage 1 is In Progress', () => pm.expect(stages[0].status).to.eql('In Progress'));
pm.test('Later stages are not started (normalized or legacy spelling)', () => {

request.description: Confirms the first live stage is active and has no mandatory fields.

exec: pm.test('Stage 1 has no mandatory fields', () => pm.expect(s.mandatory_fields === null || s.mandatory_fields === '').to.be.true);

request.description: Completes stage 1 and should auto-activate stage 2.
Checks aggregate side effects after stage 1: progress 20, current stage = Cost estimation.

exec: pm.test('Current stage moved to Cost estimation', () => pm.expect(rfq.current_stage_name).to.eql('Cost estimation'));

request.description: Reads the mandatory field requirements for Cost estimation.

item.name: [DEMO-09] Negative: advance stage 2 without mandatory data

request.description: Expected business-rule rejection. This proves mandatory-field enforcement before stage advancement.
Adds `captured_data.estimation_completed = true` so stage 2 can advance cleanly.
Completes stage 2 and should move the RFQ to Internal approval (stage 3).
Checks aggregate side effects after stage 2: progress 40, current stage = Internal approval.

exec: pm.test('Current stage moved to Internal approval', () => pm.expect(rfq.current_stage_name).to.eql('Internal approval'));

item.name: [DEMO-13] Negative: try to advance stage 4 out of sequence

request.description: Expected 409 Conflict. This proves only the current active stage can advance.

exec: pm.test('Conflict mentions current active stage', () => pm.expect(JSON.stringify(body)).to.include('current active stage'));

item.name: [DEMO-14] Add a note to current stage 3

request.description: Adds a workspace note to the current stage.
Verifies note persistence and reveals the next mandatory field (`approval_signature`).

exec: pm.test('Note is embedded in stage detail', () => pm.expect((s.notes || []).length).to.be.at.least(1));

request.description: Adds a subtask to the stage workspace.

exec: pm.test('New subtask defaults to Open', () => pm.expect(sub.status).to.eql('Open'));

request.description: Confirms the subtask is visible in the dedicated subtask list endpoint.

exec: pm.test('Created subtask is present', () => pm.expect(data.some(s => s.id === pm.environment.get('demo_subtask_id'))).to.be.true);

request.description: Marks the subtask as done.

exec: pm.test('Subtask can move to Done / 100%', () => { pm.expect(sub.status).to.eql('Done'); pm.expect(sub.progress).to.eql(100); });

item.name: [DEMO-19] Get stage 3 detail after subtask update

request.description: Verifies note + subtask are both embedded in stage detail.

exec: pm.test('Subtask is embedded in stage detail', () => pm.expect((s.subtasks || []).some(x => x.id === pm.environment.get('demo_subtask_id'))).to.be.true);

request.description: Uploads a local file to the stage. Set `demo_upload_file_path` in the environment before running this request.

exec: throw new Error('Set environment variable demo_upload_file_path to a local file path before running the upload request.');
pm.test('Uploaded file type is Other', () => pm.expect(f.type).to.eql('Other'));

request.description: Confirms the uploaded file appears in the stage file list.

exec: pm.test('Uploaded file appears in stage file list', () => pm.expect(data.some(f => f.id === pm.environment.get('demo_file_id'))).to.be.true);

request.description: Checks the file download shortcut endpoint.

exec: pm.test('Download response looks like an attachment', () => pm.expect(cd.toLowerCase()).to.include('attachment'));

request.description: Deletes the stage file by its global file ID.

item.name: [DEMO-24] Verify stage file list is empty

request.description: Confirms the file is no longer visible after deletion.

exec: pm.test('Stage files empty after delete -> 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Deletes the workspace subtask.
Confirms the subtask is no longer visible after deletion.

item.name: [DEMO-27] Negative: advance stage 3 without approval signature

request.description: Expected 422 because `approval_signature` is still missing.

exec: pm.test('Advance stage 3 without approval signature -> 422', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));

request.description: Adds `captured_data.approval_signature = true` so stage 3 can advance.
Completes stage 3 and should move the RFQ to stage 4 (Offer submission).
Checks aggregate side effects after stage 3: progress 60, current stage = Offer submission.

exec: pm.test('Current stage moved to Offer submission', () => pm.expect(rfq.current_stage_name).to.eql('Offer submission'));

item.name: [DEMO-31] Create manual reminder on current stage 4

request.description: Creates a manual reminder tied to the RFQ and current stage.

body.raw:
{
  "rfq_id": "{{demo_rfq_id}}",
  "rfq_stage_id": "{{demo_s4_id}}",
  "type": "internal",
  "message": "Manual reminder created during the Postman boss demo.",
  "due_date": "{{demo_reminder_due}}",
  "assigned_to": "Omar Chabchoub"
}

item.name: [DEMO-32] List reminders filtered by demo RFQ

request.description: Shows the reminder feed filtered to this single RFQ.

exec: pm.test('Demo reminder is returned', () => pm.expect(data.some(r => r.id === pm.environment.get('demo_reminder_id'))).to.be.true);

request.description: Validates reminder KPI counters.
Runs the reminder processing batch endpoint. With a due date of tomorrow, this often returns processed_count = 0, which is still valid.
V1 log-only reminder email smoke test.
Shows that RFQ business metadata can be updated independently of stage progression.
Checks the export/download endpoint used for reporting and offline review.

item.name: 2 — Optional Advanced Tricks & Guardrails
item.description: Extra value-add demonstrations beyond the main boss flow: invalid payload schema validation, long workflow behavior, blocker flow, skip stages, stage overrides, reminder-rule toggle. Run only if you want to go deeper.

request.description: Expected 422 schema/validation rejection. Useful to show payload guardrails.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Invalid Prefix Demo {{demo_suffix}}",
  "client": "Invalid Prefix Client {{demo_suffix}}",
  "deadline": "{{demo_deadline_short}}",
  "owner": "Omar Chabchoub",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_short_id}}",
  "industry": "Industrial Automation",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "normal",
  "description": "This request should fail because code_prefix is invalid.",
  "code_prefix": "TST"
}

request.description: Creates a second RFQ on GHI-LONG to show the 11-stage lifecycle and blocker flow.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{long_demo_rfq_name}}",
  "client": "{{long_demo_client_name}}",

  "owner": "Mohamed Guidara",

exec: pm.test('Long RFQ uses GHI long workflow', () => pm.expect(rfq.workflow_name).to.eql('GHI long workflow'));

request.description: Captures `long_s1_id` ... `long_s11_id` and proves the long workflow instantiates 11 stages.
Moves the long RFQ to stage 2 so we can demonstrate blocker handling there.
Flags stage 2 as Blocked before attempting an advance.

exec: pm.test('Set blocker on long stage 2 -> 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Expected 409 because blocker_status is Blocked.

item.name: [ADV-07] Resolve blocker + fill mandatory for long stage 2

request.description: Clears the blocker and fills the stage-2 mandatory field so the advance can succeed.
Completes the blocker flow successfully.
Creates a long-workflow RFQ while skipping template stages 3 and 6 (using workflow template IDs, not live RFQ stage IDs).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{skip_demo_rfq_name}}",
  "client": "Postman Skip Client {{demo_suffix}}",
  "deadline": "{{demo_deadline_long}}",
  "owner": "Mohamed Guidara",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_long_id}}",
  "industry": "Oil & Gas — Upstream",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "normal",
  "description": "Skip-stage demo RFQ",
  "code_prefix": "IF",
  "skip_stages": ["{{tpl_long_s3}}", "{{tpl_long_s6}}"]
}

request.description: Confirms there are only 9 live stages and that the skipped stage names are absent.

exec: pm.test('Skipping 2 template stages leaves 9 live stages', () => pm.expect(stages.length).to.eql(9));
pm.test('Live stages renumber from 1..9', () => stages.forEach((s,i) => pm.expect(s.order).to.eql(i+1)));

request.description: Creates a long-workflow RFQ and overrides the assigned team on template stage 4.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{override_demo_rfq_name}}",
  "client": "Postman Override Client {{demo_suffix}}",
  "deadline": "{{demo_deadline_long}}",
  "owner": "Mohamed Guidara",
  "workflow_id": "{{workflow_long_id}}",
  "industry": "Oil & Gas — Upstream",
  "country": "Saudi Arabia",
  "priority": "normal",
  "description": "Stage-override demo RFQ",
  "code_prefix": "IF",
  "stage_overrides": [
    {
      "stage_template_id": "{{tpl_long_s4}}",
      "assigned_team": "{{override_assigned_team}}"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Checks that the overridden team is applied on the live RFQ stage.
Optional safe admin demo for reminder-rule toggling.

item.name: 3 — Optional Diagnostics (Non-Blocking)
item.description: These requests reproduce the weak points found during earlier testing, but in a diagnostic/non-blocking way. They will not fail the run if scenario data is inconsistent; instead they log findings to the Postman console.

request.description: Loads one seeded RFQ captured during Setup so we can compare its aggregate fields against the live stage chain.

item.name: [DIAG-02] Compare seeded RFQ detail vs live stage chain

request.description: Non-blocking comparison. Logs warnings if RFQ aggregate fields (status/progress/current stage) do not align with the underlying stage chain.

exec: pm.test('Seeded RFQ stages loaded for diagnostics -> 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
if (typeof rfq.progress === 'number' && rfq.progress !== expectedProgress) issues.push(`Progress mismatch: rfq.progress=${rfq.progress}, expected≈${expectedProgress}`);
if (active && rfq.current_stage_name && rfq.current_stage_name !== active.name) issues.push(`Current stage name mismatch: rfq='${rfq.current_stage_name}', live='${active.name}'`);
if (active && rfq.current_stage_id && rfq.current_stage_id !== active.id) issues.push('Current stage id mismatch between RFQ aggregate and stage chain');
if (rfq.status && !['In preparation','Awarded','Lost','Cancelled'].includes(rfq.status)) issues.push(`RFQ aggregate status is outside the live operational set: ${rfq.status}`);

request.description: Non-blocking check across seeded + controlled responses. Logs a warning if both 'Not Started' and 'Not started' coexist.

exec: pm.test('Demo stage list for normalization diagnostics -> 200', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
if (union.includes('Not Started') && union.includes('Not started')) issues.push('Both "Not Started" and "Not started" are present across responses.');
if (issues.length) console.warn('Status normalization diagnostic issues:', issues);
